events.name: The Spinners & The Miracles
events.info: Group Sales available by calling the Genesee Box Office: 847-263-6300.
events.pleaseNote: No refunds or exchanges. Event date & time subject to change. All ages are required to have a ticket for this event. Tickets subject to cancellation if suspected of brokering or violating ticket limits. The Genesee Theatre is not responsible for any tickets that are lost, stolen, or purchased through third party websites. Any person disrupting a performance or inhibiting the enjoyment of an event for guests may be removed from the theater without refund. Unless stated everyone, regardless of age, must have a ticket to be admitted into the theater. Out of respect for our patrons, children 2 years and under are not permitted in the theater unless they attend a specific designated children's show. Backpacks and bags larger than 12" x 6" x 12" are not permitted in the auditorium. All bags are subject to search.

accessibility.info: Accessible seating for patrons with wheelchairs and their companions is available on the Orchestra, Mezzanine and Balcony levels. Elevators are located on the north side of the lobby and serve all levels. Please call (847) 263-6300 for more information.

ticketLimit.info: There is an overall 8 ticket limit for this event.

venues.name: Genesee Theatre

boxOfficeInfo.phoneNumberDetail: General Information 847-263-6300
boxOfficeInfo.openHoursDetail: Monday: CLOSED Tuesday - Friday: 11:00 am to 5:30pm Saturday: 10:00 am to 3:00 pm Sunday: Closed unless there is an event. Box Office opens 2 hours prior to show time if there is an event.
boxOfficeInfo.acceptedPaymentDetail: VISA, MASTERCARD, DISCOVER, AMERICAN EXPRESS AND CASH
boxOfficeInfo.willCallDetail: Yes, Will Call tickets available for pick up 90 minutes prior to the event. Customer must have credit card used to purchase the tickets, picture ID, and a confirmation number.

venues.parkingDetail: Ample parking is available in the city parking structure located at 225 N. County St. Please enter on County Street. A nominal fee will be charged. Valet parking is available for most events. Please approach the valet drive from the east on Clayton Street from Sheridan Road. A nominal fee will be charged.
venues.accessibleSeatingDetail: Accessible seating is available through the Box Office only. Please call 847-263-6300 Elevators are located on the north side of the lobby and serve all levels. Listening devices are available to patrons at the coat check on the first floor. A picture ID is required at the time of use.

generalInfo.generalRule: No refunds or exchanges. Event date & time subject to change. Tickets subject to cancellation if suspected of brokering or violating ticket limits. The Genesee Theatre is not responsible for any tickets that are lost, stolen, or purchased through third party website. Lobby doors open approximately 90 minutes to curtain time. Late arrivals will be seated at the discretion of the house. Any person disrupting a performance or inhibiting the enjoyment of an event for guests may be removed from the theater without a refund. Smoking, vaping, and electronic smoking are not allowed inside any part of the Genesee Theatre. Photos may be taken inside the lobby areas of the Genesee Theatre; however, cameras and recording devices are not permitted inside the auditorium unless specified by production. Professional cameras and recording devices are not permitted inside the theater. Backpacks and bags larger than 12" x 6" x 12" are not permitted in the auditorium. All bags are subject to search. For more specific information please refer to the Genesee Theatre's frequently asked questions at www.Geneseetheatre.com
generalInfo.childRule: Unless stated, everyone regardless of age, much have a ticket to be admitted into the theater. Out of respect for our patrons, children 2 years and under are not permitted in the theater unless they attend a specific designated children's show. For more information on your specific event, please refer to the event's profile at the Genesee Theatre website at www.geneseetheatre.com or by calling the Box Office at 847-263-6300

venues.name: United Center

boxOfficeInfo.openHoursDetail: Monday-Friday, 11:00 a.m. until 6:00 p.m. Saturday, closed if no performance Sunday, closed if no performance *Hours subject to change depending on event schedules. *The United Center Box Office no longer opens for event public on-sales outside normal business hours
boxOfficeInfo.acceptedPaymentDetail: The United Center Box Office accepts Cash, Visa, MasterCard, American Express and Discover Card.
boxOfficeInfo.willCallDetail: Tickets held at Will Call can be picked up beginning 90 minutes prior to the start of the actual event. Must have valid photo ID and original method of payment (if applicable) to pick up will call. Name changes are not permitted. The United Center utilizes digital ticketing for all events. Tickets must be presented for entry on a mobile device. This includes season tickets, single game tickets or individual event tickets purchased online or forwarded by email or text through authorized outlets including Chicagoblackhawks.com, Chicagobulls.com, Unitedcenter.com, Ticketmaster.com or other United Center approved outlets. Mobile entry requires a valid, rotating SafeTix barcode or digital pass added to Apple or Android Wallet. Digitally altered or fraudulent tickets will not be accepted for entry

venues.parkingDetail: The United Center has 6,000 spaces in 11 parking lots surround the arena. Parking prices will vary depending on location and event. All prices are subject to change without notice.
venues.accessibleSeatingDetail: Not only is the United Center designed to 100% ADA compliance, but each team member has been specifically trained in assisting our guests with special needs. Should you have any questions regarding the accessibility of the arena or for ticket information, please call Guest Relations at 312-455-4509 (TTY). Tickets in wheelchair accessible areas are available on all levels of the United Center, and the arena offers 100 disabled parking spaces located in parking Lot G off of Damen Avenue. All washrooms, restaurants, first aid station, seating areas, Cash Stations, box offices, Concession stands and drinking fountains are all wheelchair accessible.

generalInfo.generalRule: Cameras are permitted in the arena for sporting events, however, for concerts and family shows, cameras and flash photography are not permitted. Video cameras and other recording devices are not permitted for family shows or concerts, however, they are permitted for sporting events. Video or audio recording of the actual game-in-play is not permitted. Outside food or beverages of any kind are not permitted inside the building. Guests wearing obscene or indecent clothing are not allowed on United Center property. Guest using foul or abusive language or gestures will be escorted from the arena. In accordance with the Chicago Clean Air Act of 2005, the United Center is a smoke-free facility. Smoking of any type is not permitted anywhere inside the arena or on the United Center campus. Banners, signs, poles and sticks are not permitted inside the United Center. Laser pens and pointers are not permitted. Please remember that the United Center does have a no-readmittance policy. Policies subject to change depending on the nature of the event.
generalInfo.childRule: For all concert events, all guests, including children, are required to have a ticket to enter the arena. For all professional and collegiate sporting events as well as Feld Entertainment family shows, children that are under 36 inches in height do not need a ticket to enter the arena. The adult will be allowed to have one child sit on his/her lap.

venues.name: United Center

boxOfficeInfo.openHoursDetail: Monday-Friday, 11:00 a.m. until 6:00 p.m. Saturday, closed if no performance Sunday, closed if no performance *Hours subject to change depending on event schedules. *The United Center Box Office no longer opens for event public on-sales outside normal business hours
boxOfficeInfo.acceptedPaymentDetail: The United Center Box Office accepts Cash, Visa, MasterCard, American Express and Discover Card.
boxOfficeInfo.willCallDetail: Tickets held at Will Call can be picked up beginning 90 minutes prior to the start of the actual event. Must have valid photo ID and original method of payment (if applicable) to pick up will call. Name changes are not permitted. The United Center utilizes digital ticketing for all events. Tickets must be presented for entry on a mobile device. This includes season tickets, single game tickets or individual event tickets purchased online or forwarded by email or text through authorized outlets including Chicagoblackhawks.com, Chicagobulls.com, Unitedcenter.com, Ticketmaster.com or other United Center approved outlets. Mobile entry requires a valid, rotating SafeTix barcode or digital pass added to Apple or Android Wallet. Digitally altered or fraudulent tickets will not be accepted for entry

venues.parkingDetail: The United Center has 6,000 spaces in 11 parking lots surround the arena. Parking prices will vary depending on location and event. All prices are subject to change without notice.
venues.accessibleSeatingDetail: Not only is the United Center designed to 100% ADA compliance, but each team member has been specifically trained in assisting our guests with special needs. Should you have any questions regarding the accessibility of the arena or for ticket information, please call Guest Relations at 312-455-4509 (TTY). Tickets in wheelchair accessible areas are available on all levels of the United Center, and the arena offers 100 disabled parking spaces located in parking Lot G off of Damen Avenue. All washrooms, restaurants, first aid station, seating areas, Cash Stations, box offices, Concession stands and drinking fountains are all wheelchair accessible.

generalInfo.generalRule: Cameras are permitted in the arena for sporting events, however, for concerts and family shows, cameras and flash photography are not permitted. Video cameras and other recording devices are not permitted for family shows or concerts, however, they are permitted for sporting events. Video or audio recording of the actual game-in-play is not permitted. Outside food or beverages of any kind are not permitted inside the building. Guests wearing obscene or indecent clothing are not allowed on United Center property. Guest using foul or abusive language or gestures will be escorted from the arena. In accordance with the Chicago Clean Air Act of 2005, the United Center is a smoke-free facility. Smoking of any type is not permitted anywhere inside the arena or on the United Center campus. Banners, signs, poles and sticks are not permitted inside the United Center. Laser pens and pointers are not permitted. Please remember that the United Center does have a no-readmittance policy. Policies subject to change depending on the nature of the event.
generalInfo.childRule: For all concert events, all guests, including children, are required to have a ticket to enter the arena. For all professional and collegiate sporting events as well as Feld Entertainment family shows, children that are under 36 inches in height do not need a ticket to enter the arena. The adult will be allowed to have one child sit on his/her lap.

venues.name: United Center

boxOfficeInfo.openHoursDetail: Monday-Friday, 11:00 a.m. until 6:00 p.m. Saturday, closed if no performance Sunday, closed if no performance *Hours subject to change depending on event schedules. *The United Center Box Office no longer opens for event public on-sales outside normal business hours
boxOfficeInfo.acceptedPaymentDetail: The United Center Box Office accepts Cash, Visa, MasterCard, American Express and Discover Card.
boxOfficeInfo.willCallDetail: Tickets held at Will Call can be picked up beginning 90 minutes prior to the start of the actual event. Must have valid photo ID and original method of payment (if applicable) to pick up will call. Name changes are not permitted. The United Center utilizes digital ticketing for all events. Tickets must be presented for entry on a mobile device. This includes season tickets, single game tickets or individual event tickets purchased online or forwarded by email or text through authorized outlets including Chicagoblackhawks.com, Chicagobulls.com, Unitedcenter.com, Ticketmaster.com or other United Center approved outlets. Mobile entry requires a valid, rotating SafeTix barcode or digital pass added to Apple or Android Wallet. Digitally altered or fraudulent tickets will not be accepted for entry

venues.parkingDetail: The United Center has 6,000 spaces in 11 parking lots surround the arena. Parking prices will vary depending on location and event. All prices are subject to change without notice.
venues.accessibleSeatingDetail: Not only is the United Center designed to 100% ADA compliance, but each team member has been specifically trained in assisting our guests with special needs. Should you have any questions regarding the accessibility of the arena or for ticket information, please call Guest Relations at 312-455-4509 (TTY). Tickets in wheelchair accessible areas are available on all levels of the United Center, and the arena offers 100 disabled parking spaces located in parking Lot G off of Damen Avenue. All washrooms, restaurants, first aid station, seating areas, Cash Stations, box offices, Concession stands and drinking fountains are all wheelchair accessible.

generalInfo.generalRule: Cameras are permitted in the arena for sporting events, however, for concerts and family shows, cameras and flash photography are not permitted. Video cameras and other recording devices are not permitted for family shows or concerts, however, they are permitted for sporting events. Video or audio recording of the actual game-in-play is not permitted. Outside food or beverages of any kind are not permitted inside the building. Guests wearing obscene or indecent clothing are not allowed on United Center property. Guest using foul or abusive language or gestures will be escorted from the arena. In accordance with the Chicago Clean Air Act of 2005, the United Center is a smoke-free facility. Smoking of any type is not permitted anywhere inside the arena or on the United Center campus. Banners, signs, poles and sticks are not permitted inside the United Center. Laser pens and pointers are not permitted. Please remember that the United Center does have a no-readmittance policy. Policies subject to change depending on the nature of the event.
generalInfo.childRule: For all concert events, all guests, including children, are required to have a ticket to enter the arena. For all professional and collegiate sporting events as well as Feld Entertainment family shows, children that are under 36 inches in height do not need a ticket to enter the arena. The adult will be allowed to have one child sit on his/her lap.

boxOfficeInfo.openHoursDetail: Box office hours vary. Please call (312) 977-1700 for the current week's hours.
boxOfficeInfo.acceptedPaymentDetail: The Box Office accepts Cash, Visa, Mastercard, American Express and Discover. No personal checks, please.
boxOfficeInfo.willCallDetail: Will Call tickets can be picked up at any time during Box Office hours on the day of a show. The customer must present the actual credit card used to make the order, a valid picture ID, and the confirmation number.

venues.parkingDetail: Reduced rate parking is available at 17 East Adams.
venues.accessibleSeatingDetail: For wheelchair accessible seating please call the box office at 312-977-1700 ext. 1259. ELEVATOR: There is no elevator access to the dress circle level of the theatre. generalInfo.generalRule: There are no refunds or exchanges on tickets purchased for Broadway In Chicago events. Cameras and recording devices are not allowed. Doors open 30 minutes prior to showtime. No outside food or beverages allowed.
generalInfo.childRule: For most productions children under 5 are not permitted for the courtesy of the other patrons. Everyone is required to have a ticket.

If there is a larger request or if there are any inquiries about accessible seating, please contact Laura Arenas at the Rate Field Box Office at 312-674-1000-ext. 5246. Accessible elevators are located at Gate 4 entrance and at Gate 5.

generalInfo.generalRule: Guest are permitted to bring food items into the park in see-through plastic bags that can be stored underneath the seats. No bottles cans or beverages other than water are allowed.
generalInfo.childRule: Children who are taller than the arm of the turnstile (approximately 36 inches) require a ticket of their own. Children smaller than the arm of the turnstile do not require a ticket of their own, but they must sit on the lap of an accompanying adult.
